name: Millworks Technologies IPO
verdictReason: Estimated GMP-based premium is 119.3%. IPOweb Data Score is 6.1/10 based on a 100-point evidence model. Data confidence is HIGH. GMP trend is rising.
overview: Millworks Technologies Limited is a precision engineering company engaged in manufacturing machined components, sheet metal parts and integrated assemblies for sectors including railways, aerospace, defence and semiconductors. The company operates through Build-to-Print (BTP) and Build-to-Spec (BTS) manufacturing models, producing components based on customer drawings, technical specifications and functional requirements.
financials: For the period ended 30 November 2025, Millworks Technologies reported total income of ₹68.02 crore and profit after tax of ₹20.50 crore. In FY2025, total income was ₹22.42 crore with PAT of ₹5.25 crore, compared with ₹9.40 crore total income and ₹1.95 crore PAT in FY2024. The reported financials indicate rapid growth in revenue and profitability, while total borrowings increased to ₹16.67 crore as of November 2025.
gmpTrend: Millworks Technologies IPO GMP momentum is currently positive but volatile. The tracked GMP increased from ₹300 on 14 July 2026 to ₹395 on 15 July 2026, indicating strengthening grey market sentiment. However, GMP is unofficial and can change rapidly; therefore, the trend should be evaluated together with final subscription demand and institutional QIB participation.
strengths: Strong revenue and profit growth, exposure to high-value sectors such as aerospace, defence, railways and semiconductors, and capabilities across precision machining, sheet metal fabrication and integrated assemblies are key strengths. The Build-to-Print and Build-to-Spec manufacturing models support customized engineering requirements.
risks: Key risks include a short operating history, high customer concentration, negative operating cash flow and a working-capital-intensive business model. The company also faces SME listing liquidity and price volatility risk. Its rapid recent growth may not be sustainable at the same pace, while concentration of manufacturing operations in Bengaluru can create operational concentration risk.

GMP Trend Analysis
The Grey Market Premium (GMP) remained volatile during the IPO period and declined sharply before listing. Despite some short-lived improvement, the premium failed to sustain higher levels, indicating weak demand in the grey market. The current GMP suggests only a flat to marginally positive listing, reflecting cautious investor sentiment.

Growing textile manufacturing business.
Attractive valuation compared with several listed peers.
Capacity expansion planned.
Healthy operating history.
risks:
Textile industry is cyclical.
Margin pressure from raw material costs.
GMP indicates only a muted listing expectation.
Demand from institutional investors was not exceptionally strong. �
